strengths: Comprehensive AnalyticsEvent enum with 77+ events covering auth, billing, onboarding, admin, engagement, and PQL signals
Frontend/backend event parity enforced by AnalyticsEventSyncTest
Type-safe frontend event properties via EventPropertyMap with compile-time exhaustiveness check
PII sanitization (34-key blocklist) in AnalyticsGateway before GA4 forwarding
GDPR-compliant consent gate: explicit decline honored, legitimate interest for unset consent
EmailSendLog deduplication prevents double lifecycle emails with unique constraint + race-safe record()
First-touch UTM attribution via session-based middleware
Lifecycle stage machine with audit trail, cache invalidation, and automatic win-back dispatch
Batch-optimized scoring services (EngagementScoringService.scoreBatch uses 4 queries for N users)
GA4 Measurement Protocol integration via async job with retry and PII filtering
critical_issues_summary: CustomerHealthService queries 'login' but AuditService logs 'auth.login' — loginFrequencyScore is always 0, deflating all health and lead scores
StripeWebhookController logs raw Stripe events to audit_logs without dispatching AnalyticsEvent enums — payment failure/recovery analytics are invisible to GA4 and ProductAnalyticsService

findings.title: CustomerHealthService login event name mismatch — scores always 0
findings.description: CustomerHealthService.loginFrequencyScore() queries audit_logs with ->where('event', 'login') on lines 237 and 261, but AuditService.logLogin() persists events as 'auth.login' (AnalyticsEvent::AUTH_LOGIN->value). The query never matches any rows.
findings.impact: Login frequency score is always 0 for every user, deflating health scores by up to 25 points. Lead scores (weighted 0.3 on health) are systematically understated. Health distribution dashboard inflates 'critical' and 'at_risk' buckets. MQL/SQL lead qualification thresholds are harder to reach, suppressing upgrade nudges.
findings.fix: Change ->where('event', 'login') to ->where('event', 'auth.login') on lines 237 and 261 of CustomerHealthService.php

findings.title: Stripe webhook handlers bypass AnalyticsEvent dispatch entirely
findings.description: StripeWebhookController logs all webhook events via logWebhookEvent() which calls AuditService::log() with raw Stripe event names (e.g. 'stripe.invoice.payment_failed') instead of AnalyticsEvent enum values. These events are not in GA4_FORWARDED_EVENTS and are invisible to ProductAnalyticsService queries that filter on enum values like 'subscription.created'.
findings.impact: Payment failures, payment recoveries, refunds, subscription updates, and trial-will-end events from Stripe are logged to audit_logs with 'stripe.*' prefix but never dispatched as AnalyticsEvent enum values. ProductAnalyticsService.getSubscriptionEvents() counts 'subscription.created' but webhook-originated subscription creates are logged as 'stripe.subscription.created' — different key, not counted. GA4 never receives payment failure or recovery signals.
findings.fix: In webhook handlers, dispatch the corresponding AnalyticsEvent enum in addition to the stripe.* audit log. E.g., in handleInvoicePaymentFailed, also call AuditService::log(AnalyticsEvent::BILLING_PAYMENT_FAILED, ...) and consider adding payment failure events to GA4_FORWARDED_EVENTS.

findings.title: retention_coupon_applied uses raw string instead of AnalyticsEvent enum
findings.description: SubscriptionController::applyRetentionCoupon() logs 'retention_coupon_applied' as a raw string to AuditService::log(), bypassing the AnalyticsEvent enum. No corresponding enum case exists.
findings.impact: Retention coupon analytics cannot be queried via typed event name. Event is invisible to frontend sync test and any analytics queries filtering on AnalyticsEvent enum values.
findings.fix: Add BILLING_RETENTION_COUPON_APPLIED case to AnalyticsEvent enum and use it in the controller.

findings.title: BILLING_TRIAL_UPGRADE_CLICKED defined but never fired
findings.description: AnalyticsEvent::BILLING_TRIAL_UPGRADE_CLICKED is defined in both PHP enum and frontend events.ts with type-safe properties, but grep shows no actual fire/track call in any component or controller. Only appears in definition and sync test files.
findings.impact: Trial upgrade funnel has a dead spot — cannot measure how many trial users click the upgrade CTA. This is a key conversion metric that's defined but uncollected.
findings.fix: Add track(AnalyticsEvents.BILLING_TRIAL_UPGRADE_CLICKED, { tier }) call to the upgrade CTA in the Billing/Index.tsx page for trial users.

findings.title: SALES_INQUIRY_SUBMITTED defined but never fired from frontend
findings.description: AnalyticsEvents.SALES_INQUIRY_SUBMITTED is defined in events.ts but grep shows no track() call in any .tsx component. Only appears in definition and sync test.
findings.impact: Enterprise sales inquiry submissions are not tracked in frontend analytics. Backend may log it server-side, but frontend-originated GA4 event is missing.
findings.fix: Add track() call in the enterprise/sales inquiry form component.

findings.title: Webhook audit events use stripe.* prefix creating parallel event namespace
findings.description: logWebhookEvent() prepends 'stripe.' to all webhook events (e.g. 'stripe.subscription.created', 'stripe.invoice.payment_failed'). This creates a parallel namespace to the AnalyticsEvent enum values. ProductAnalyticsService counts 'subscription.created' (from controller-side audit) but webhook-originated subscriptions are logged as 'stripe.subscription.created'.
findings.impact: Subscription counts in ProductAnalyticsService may undercount events that only arrive via webhook (e.g., subscriptions created via Stripe Dashboard or API directly). Admin dashboard subscription event counts are unreliable.
findings.fix: Either (a) query both event names in ProductAnalyticsService, or (b) dispatch the canonical AnalyticsEvent alongside the stripe.* audit log.

findings.title: Payment failure and recovery events not forwarded to GA4
findings.description: GA4_FORWARDED_EVENTS list includes subscription.created and subscription.canceled but not billing.payment_failed or any payment recovery event. Payment failures are a key revenue signal for GA4 audience segmentation.
findings.impact: GA4 cannot segment users by payment failure status. Cannot build payment recovery audiences or measure dunning effectiveness in GA4.
findings.fix: Consider adding 'billing.payment_failed' and a new 'billing.payment_recovered' event to GA4_FORWARDED_EVENTS after the webhook handler dispatches them as AnalyticsEvent enums.

findings.title: ResumeSubscriptionDialog does not fire frontend analytics event
findings.description: CancelSubscriptionDialog fires BILLING_SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELED but ResumeSubscriptionDialog does not fire BILLING_SUBSCRIPTION_RESUMED. The event is defined in events.ts but not tracked on the frontend resume action.
findings.impact: Frontend resume analytics are missing. Server-side SUBSCRIPTION_RESUMED is still logged, but frontend funnel from dialog interaction to completion is incomplete.
findings.fix: Add track(AnalyticsEvents.BILLING_SUBSCRIPTION_RESUMED) call in ResumeSubscriptionDialog's onSuccess handler.

findings.title: ProductAnalyticsService activation rate uses different method than CustomerHealthService
findings.description: ProductAnalyticsService.getActivationRate() counts user_settings rows with key='onboarding_completed'. CustomerHealthService.getActivationRate() counts users who verified email within 7 days. These are fundamentally different metrics both called 'activation rate'.
findings.impact: Admin dashboard may show inconsistent activation rates depending on which service provides the data. Two metrics named the same thing measuring different behaviors creates confusion.
findings.fix: Rename one metric to disambiguate: e.g., 'onboarding_completion_rate' (ProductAnalyticsService) vs 'email_activation_rate' (CustomerHealthService).

findings.title: LifecycleService has no unit tests
findings.description: LifecycleService.transition() handles stage transitions, audit logging, cache invalidation, and win-back notification dispatch — but has no dedicated test file. It may be indirectly tested via integration tests but core transition logic is unverified.
findings.impact: Regression risk on stage transition logic, days_in_previous_stage calculation, no-op same-stage check, and automatic win-back dispatch.
findings.fix: Create tests/Unit/Services/LifecycleServiceTest.php covering: transition happy path, no-op same stage, days calculation, cache invalidation, win-back on CHURNED, audit log dispatch.

findings.title: LeadScoringService has no unit tests
findings.description: LeadScoringService.score() computes composite lead scores using engagement, health, and PQL signals — but has no dedicated test file.
findings.impact: Regression risk on score formula, threshold constants, PQL signal detection from audit_logs. Since health scores are already broken (ANA-001), lead scores are systematically wrong and no test catches this.
findings.fix: Create tests/Unit/Services/LeadScoringServiceTest.php covering: composite formula, MQL/SQL thresholds, PQL signal from limit.threshold_80 events.

findings.title: Missing command tests for 5 lifecycle commands
findings.description: SendDunningReminders, SendOnboardingReminders, CheckExpiredTrials, ComputeUserScores, and QualifyLeads commands lack dedicated test files.
findings.impact: No regression protection for email deduplication in dunning sequence, onboarding timing logic, expired trial transition, batch score computation, or lead qualification output.
findings.fix: Create Feature test files for each command.

findings.title: LifecycleService uses saveQuietly — model events suppressed
findings.description: LifecycleService::transition() calls $user->saveQuietly() which suppresses model events. If any observer or event listener depends on User 'updated' events for the lifecycle_stage column, they won't fire.
findings.impact: Any model observer watching for lifecycle_stage changes on the User model will be silently bypassed. This is likely intentional to prevent event loops but is undocumented.
findings.fix: Add a code comment explaining why saveQuietly() is used (prevent observer loops). If observers should fire, switch to save().

findings.title: lifecycle.transition is a raw string, not an AnalyticsEvent enum member
findings.description: LifecycleService logs 'lifecycle.transition' as a raw string to AuditService::log(). This event is not in the AnalyticsEvent enum and has no frontend counterpart.
findings.impact: Lifecycle transitions are logged to audit_logs but don't participate in the typed analytics system. If lifecycle transition events need GA4 forwarding in the future, they'd need an enum case. The raw string approach means no compile-time or sync-test enforcement.
findings.fix: Consider adding LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION to AnalyticsEvent enum if this event should be queryable alongside other analytics events.

findings.title: GA4 batch endpoint (sendBatch) never called
findings.description: AnalyticsGateway.sendBatch() supports batching up to 25 events per GA4 request, but DispatchAnalyticsEvent.handle() always calls send() for single events. The batch method appears unused.
findings.impact: Each GA4-forwarded event makes a separate HTTP request. For users triggering multiple GA4 events in quick succession (e.g., register + trial.started), this creates unnecessary HTTP overhead.
findings.fix: Consider a batching job that collects events within a time window and sends them via sendBatch(). Low priority since only 11 event types are GA4-forwarded.

findings.title: LifecycleService stage velocity SQL uses AVG but description says median
findings.description: getStageVelocity() docblock says 'Get median days between stage pairs' but the SQL uses AVG(), which computes the mean. Outliers (e.g., a user stuck in trial for 200 days) will skew the result.
findings.impact: Admin dashboard stage velocity metrics are mean values presented as if they were medians. Outlier stage durations disproportionately affect the displayed values.
findings.fix: Either change the docblock to say 'average' or implement actual median calculation.

findings.title: analytics-thresholds.php has minimal scope
findings.description: Config only defines 3 thresholds (churn_rate, mrr_drop_percent, trial_conversion). Engagement score brackets, health score brackets, and lead qualification thresholds are hardcoded in service classes rather than configurable.
findings.impact: Adjusting scoring thresholds requires code changes rather than config/env updates. Low impact since these change rarely.
findings.fix: Consider moving MQL_THRESHOLD, SQL_THRESHOLD, and health score brackets to analytics-thresholds.php.

findings.title: 14 notification classes lack dedicated tests
findings.description: AdminHealthAlertNotification, ExpansionNudgeNotification, IncompletePaymentReminder, InvoluntaryChurnWinBackNotification, LimitThresholdNotification, PaymentActionRequiredNotification, PaymentFailedNotification, PaymentRecoveredNotification, RefundProcessedNotification, UpgradeNudgeNotification, WinBackNotification, TrialEndingNotification, AdminAnnouncementNotification, SalesInquiryNotification lack dedicated test files.
findings.impact: Notification content, formatting, and routing are unverified. Some are indirectly tested via command tests.
findings.fix: Prioritize tests for payment-related notifications (PaymentFailedNotification, PaymentRecoveredNotification, InvoluntaryChurnWinBackNotification).

findings.title: UTM data captured in session but persistence to user record is controller-dependent
findings.description: CaptureUtmParameters middleware stores UTM data in session, but persistence to the user model depends on RegisteredUserController explicitly reading it. If registration flows bypass this controller (e.g., social auth), UTM attribution may be lost.
findings.impact: Social auth registrations may not capture UTM attribution data. First-touch attribution is incomplete for OAuth signups.
findings.fix: Verify that social auth registration also persists session UTM data to the user record.

findings.title: useAnalytics hook catches usePage() errors for non-Inertia contexts
findings.description: The hook wraps usePage() in try-catch to handle non-Inertia rendering contexts (unit tests). The eslint-disable for rules-of-hooks is documented but unconventional.
findings.impact: Minimal — this is a known pattern documented in the code. The eslint-disable is necessary for the conditional hook behavior.
findings.fix: No action needed — well-documented trade-off.

findings.title: EngagementScoringService token threshold differs from CustomerHealthService
findings.description: EngagementScoringService awards depth bonus at tokenCount > 4 (line 149), but CustomerHealthService awards depth bonus at tokens_count >= 5 (line 295). The comment in CustomerHealthService says weights 'mirror' EngagementScoringService, but the threshold is off-by-one.
findings.impact: Minor scoring inconsistency: a user with exactly 5 tokens gets the depth bonus from both services, but the threshold semantics differ (> 4 vs >= 5 produces identical results). Functionally equivalent but confusing for maintainers.
findings.fix: Align to the same comparison operator for clarity.

findings.title: No analytics events for lifecycle email sends
findings.description: Lifecycle email commands (SendTrialNudges, SendDunningReminders, SendTrialEndingReminders, SendOnboardingReminders) send notifications but don't dispatch analytics events for 'email_sent' actions. EmailSendLog records the send but no audit trail exists for analytics queries.
findings.impact: Cannot query audit_logs to measure email send rates, delivery timing, or sequence progression in admin analytics dashboard. EmailSendLog provides dedup but not analytics visibility.
findings.fix: Consider dispatching a 'lifecycle.email_sent' event with sequence_type and email_number metadata when each lifecycle email is sent.

AnalyticsGateway.purpose: GA4 Measurement Protocol sender with PII sanitization

AuditService.purpose: Central event logger with GA4 forwarding, consent gate, PII protection
AuditService.notes: Logs to audit_logs table via PersistAuditLog job and optionally forwards to GA4 via DispatchAnalyticsEvent

LifecycleService.purpose: User lifecycle stage transitions with audit trail and win-back dispatch

EngagementScoringService.purpose: 0-100 engagement score from login recency, feature adoption, onboarding, account age

CustomerHealthService.purpose: 0-100 health score from login frequency, feature adoption, billing status, profile completion
CustomerHealthService.notes: CRITICAL BUG: loginFrequencyScore queries wrong event name (ANA-001)

LeadScoringService.notes: MQL>=60, SQL>=80, PQL signal from limit.threshold_80 events

ProductAnalyticsService.purpose: Signup trends, onboarding funnel, activation rate, feature adoption, subscription events

DispatchAnalyticsEvent.purpose: Async GA4 dispatch with 2 retries and exponential backoff

CaptureUtmParameters.purpose: First-touch UTM attribution via session storage

ga4_forwarding_matrix.consent_mechanism: GDPR Art. 6(1)(f) legitimate interest for unset consent; explicit decline (false) always honored
ga4_forwarding_matrix.pii_protection: 34-key blocklist stripped before GA4 send; IP anonymized for non-security events when configured

immediate.action: Fix CustomerHealthService event name from 'login' to 'auth.login'
immediate.impact: Restores health scoring accuracy for all users
Brings retention coupon tracking into typed analytics system

short_term.action: Dispatch AnalyticsEvent enums from Stripe webhook handlers alongside stripe.* audit logs
short_term.impact: Makes payment lifecycle visible in GA4 and ProductAnalyticsService
Regression protection for critical state machine
Verifies composite scoring formula and threshold logic

medium_term.action: Create test files for 5 untested lifecycle commands
medium_term.impact: Regression protection for email sequences and scoring pipelines

medium_term.action: Expand GA4_FORWARDED_EVENTS to include payment failure and trial conversion
medium_term.impact: Enables GA4 audience segmentation by payment status

medium_term.action: Disambiguate activation rate metrics across services
